subject: current-affairs
description: Uno & Other Organizations MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is the duration of the term of the United Nations Secretary-General?
mcqs.explanation: The UN Secretary-General is appointed for a 5-year term, renewable once.

mcqs.q: The UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres belongs to which country?
mcqs.explanation: Antonio Guterres, the 9th UN Secretary-General, is from Portugal.

mcqs.q: How many delegates can each country send to the UN General Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: Each member state may send up to 5 delegates to the UN General Assembly.

mcqs.q: Who was the first Secretary-General elected from the regular staff (career staff) of the United Nations?
mcqs.explanation: Kofi Annan was the first SG to be elected from the UN's own career staff.

mcqs.q: Pakistan became a full member of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan and India became full SCO members on 9 June 2017 at the Astana summit.

mcqs.q: Which nation has become the new member of NATO?
mcqs.explanation: Sweden joined NATO in 2024, becoming its 32nd member after Finland.

mcqs.q: What are the total members of NATO?
mcqs.explanation: NATO currently has 32 member states (including Finland and Sweden).

mcqs.q: How many countries are members of the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: The European Union has 27 member states after the UK's withdrawal.

mcqs.q: The term of the President of the UN General Assembly is:
mcqs.explanation: The President of the UN General Assembly is elected for a one-year term.

mcqs.q: When was 'The United Nations Declaration' signed?
mcqs.explanation: The 'Declaration by United Nations' was signed in Washington on 1 January 1942 by 26 nations.

mcqs.q: When was the United Nations set up?
mcqs.explanation: The UN officially came into existence on 24 October 1945 when the Charter entered force — observed every year as UN Day.

mcqs.q: Who signed the 'United Nations Declaration'?
options: President Roosevelt of America, Winston Churchill PM of Britain
mcqs.explanation: All four — Roosevelt, Churchill, Litvinov and Soong — signed the original Declaration by United Nations on 1 January 1942.

mcqs.q: Currently how many states have observer status in the UN?
mcqs.explanation: There are currently 2 UN Observer States: the Holy See (Vatican) and the State of Palestine.

mcqs.q: The term 'United Nations' was coined by:
mcqs.explanation: US President Franklin D. Roosevelt coined the term 'United Nations' in 1942.

mcqs.q: With the addition of South Sudan on 13 July 2011, total UN member states are:
mcqs.explanation: South Sudan became the 193rd UN member state on 14 July 2011.

mcqs.q: The United Nations headquarters is situated in:
mcqs.explanation: The UN headquarters is in New York City, USA.

mcqs.q: Who was the first Secretary-General of the United Nations?
mcqs.explanation: Trygve Lie of Norway was the first UN Secretary-General (1946–1952).

mcqs.q: Name the only Secretary-General of the United Nations who resigned from his post.
mcqs.explanation: Trygve Lie is the only UN Secretary-General who resigned (in 1952).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a permanent member of the Security Council?
mcqs.explanation: Germany is not a permanent member of the Security Council.

mcqs.q: The Security Council has 15 members, 5 of which are permanent. Which of the following is NOT a permanent member?
mcqs.explanation: Germany is not a permanent member; the P5 are China, France, UK, US, and Russia.

mcqs.q: Which of these countries was NOT an original member of the UN?
mcqs.explanation: Italy was NOT one of the 51 original UN members.

mcqs.q: In which city was the United Nations created?
mcqs.explanation: The UN was created at the San Francisco Conference in 1945.

mcqs.q: Where is the head office of the UN High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR)?
mcqs.explanation: UNHCR is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Where does the International Court of Justice sit?
mcqs.explanation: The ICJ sits at the Peace Palace in The Hague, Netherlands.

mcqs.q: Where is the UN International Crime and Justice Research Institute (UNICRI) located?
mcqs.explanation: UNICRI is headquartered in Turin, Italy.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) located?
mcqs.explanation: UNEP is headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya.

mcqs.q: Where is the World Health Organization (WHO) based?
mcqs.explanation: The WHO is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: What is the foremost important purpose of the UN?
mcqs.explanation: The maintenance of international peace and security is the UN's primary purpose under Article 1 of the Charter.

mcqs.q: When the San Francisco Conference adopted the UN Charter, how many nations signed the said Charter?
mcqs.explanation: 50 nations signed the UN Charter at the San Francisco Conference; Poland signed later, becoming an original member.

mcqs.q: Where is the International Criminal Court located?
mcqs.explanation: The International Criminal Court is located in The Hague, Netherlands.

mcqs.q: What are the principal organs of the UN?
options: The General Assembly, The Security Council
The Economic and Social Council, The Trusteeship Council
The International Court of Justice, The Secretariat
mcqs.explanation: The UN has six principal organs — all listed in options A–C.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the World Meteorological Organization?
mcqs.explanation: The WMO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: What majority is required of UN member states for decisions on important issues?
mcqs.explanation: Decisions on important questions require a two-thirds majority of those present and voting.

mcqs.q: Who appoints the Secretary-General of the UN?
options: UN General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council
mcqs.explanation: The Secretary-General is appointed by the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD)?
mcqs.explanation: IFAD is headquartered in Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: Who elects the ten non-permanent members of the Security Council?
mcqs.explanation: The 10 non-permanent members of the UNSC are elected by the General Assembly.

mcqs.q: How many members are there in the Security Council?
mcqs.explanation: The UNSC has 15 members in total.

mcqs.q: How many permanent members exist in the Security Council?
mcqs.explanation: There are 5 permanent members (P5) of the UN Security Council.

mcqs.q: How many non-permanent members are in the Security Council?
mcqs.explanation: The UNSC has 10 non-permanent members.

mcqs.q: Who are the permanent members of the Security Council?
options: Japan, Britain, America, Russia and France
China, Britain, America, Russia and Germany
China, Britain, America, Russia and France
China, Britain, America, Japan and France
mcqs.explanation: The five permanent members are China, Britain, America, Russia and France.

mcqs.q: On what date did the Security Council come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: The Security Council held its first session on 17 January 1946; it came into existence on 12 January 1946.

mcqs.q: For what period are the ten non-permanent members elected by the UN General Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: Non-permanent UNSC members serve 2-year terms.

mcqs.q: When was the UN Children's Fund (UNICEF) established?
mcqs.explanation: UNICEF was established on 11 December 1946.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO)?
mcqs.explanation: ICAO is headquartered in Montreal, Canada.

mcqs.q: When was the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) established?
mcqs.explanation: UNEP was established in June 1972 following the Stockholm Conference.

mcqs.q: The Charter of the United Nations consists of:
mcqs.explanation: The UN Charter has 111 articles divided into 19 chapters.

mcqs.q: Where is the UN International Drug Control Programme (UNODC) headquarters located?
mcqs.explanation: UNODC is headquartered in Vienna, Austria.

mcqs.q: To which UN organ does the Security Council send its annual report?
mcqs.explanation: The Security Council submits an annual report to the General Assembly.

mcqs.q: The United Nations Secretariat consists of:
mcqs.explanation: The UN Secretariat is structured into Departments and Offices headed by the Secretary-General.

mcqs.q: Which of the following was established at the Bretton Woods Conference in 1944?
mcqs.explanation: The IMF (and the World Bank) were established at the 1944 Bretton Woods Conference.

mcqs.q: When was the International Labour Organization (ILO) established?
mcqs.explanation: The ILO was established in 1919 under the Treaty of Versailles.

mcqs.q: Which organization promotes free trade between Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay?
mcqs.explanation: Mercosur is the Southern Common Market of Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay (with Venezuela's status varying).

mcqs.q: The Second World Conference on Human Rights was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 1993 Second World Conference on Human Rights was held in Vienna, Austria.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Telecommunications Union (ITU)?
mcqs.explanation: ITU is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of UNESCO?
mcqs.explanation: UNESCO is headquartered in Paris, France.

mcqs.q: When was the Universal Postal Union (UPU) established?
mcqs.explanation: The UPU was established in 1874 in Bern.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Universal Postal Union?
mcqs.explanation: UPU is headquartered in Bern, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization?
mcqs.explanation: WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO)?
mcqs.explanation: WIPO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When was the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) statute approved by the UN?
mcqs.explanation: The IAEA Statute was approved on 26 October 1956 and came into force on 29 July 1957.

mcqs.q: When did the UN General Assembly adopt the Universal Declaration of Human Rights?
mcqs.explanation: The UDHR was adopted on 10 December 1948 — observed worldwide as Human Rights Day.

mcqs.q: What was the 'spark' to the First World War?
mcqs.explanation: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo on 28 June 1914 sparked WWI.

mcqs.q: When was the League of Nations formed?
mcqs.explanation: The League of Nations came into being on 10 January 1920 after the Treaty of Versailles.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the League of Nations?
mcqs.explanation: The League of Nations was headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: The idea of the League of Nations was drafted by which US President?
mcqs.explanation: US President Woodrow Wilson drafted the idea of the League of Nations in his Fourteen Points (1918).

mcqs.q: Where are the headquarters of the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: EU institutions are split between Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg.

mcqs.q: Which country has become the first-ever nation to leave the European Union?
mcqs.explanation: The United Kingdom became the first country to leave the EU through Brexit (effective 31 January 2020).

mcqs.q: When was the European Monetary System (EMS) launched?
mcqs.explanation: The European Monetary System was launched in March 1979.

mcqs.q: When did Euro banknotes and coins start to circulate?
mcqs.explanation: Euro banknotes and coins entered circulation on 1 January 2002.

mcqs.q: Where is the European Central Bank (ECB) located?
mcqs.explanation: The European Central Bank is headquartered in Frankfurt, Germany.

mcqs.q: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) was established in:
mcqs.explanation: NATO was established by the North Atlantic Treaty signed on 4 April 1949.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a military alliance?
mcqs.explanation: NATO is the only military alliance among the listed options.

mcqs.q: Where are the NATO headquarters?
mcqs.explanation: NATO's headquarters is in Brussels, Belgium.

mcqs.q: The Warsaw Pact was founded on _____ and consisted of the Soviet Union and its European allies.
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Treaty was signed on 14 May 1955 in Warsaw, Poland.

mcqs.q: The headquarters of the Warsaw Pact was in:
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was headquartered in Moscow, USSR.

mcqs.q: When did Turkey, Iran and Pakistan withdraw from CENTO?
mcqs.explanation: After the Iranian Revolution, CENTO was dissolved in 1979 with Turkey, Iran and Pakistan withdrawing.

mcqs.q: SEATO was formally founded in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was formally founded in 1955 (Manila Treaty signed in 1954).

mcqs.q: SEATO was founded by the Pact of:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was created by the Manila Pact (Southeast Asia Collective Defense Treaty) of 1954.

mcqs.q: SEATO's headquarters were in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was headquartered in Bangkok, Thailand.

mcqs.q: Where is the seat of the African Union (AU)?
mcqs.explanation: The seat of the African Union is in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

mcqs.q: When did the African Union come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: The African Union was established on 26 May 2001 (Constitutive Act adopted 11 July 2000).

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the African Union?
mcqs.explanation: The AU's headquarters is in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The African Development Bank (AfDB) is headquartered in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire.

mcqs.q: In which city is the Red Cross based?
mcqs.explanation: The International Red Cross is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When was the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) established by Jean Henri Dunant?
mcqs.explanation: The ICRC was founded in Geneva in February 1863 by Jean Henri Dunant.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Committee of the Red Cross?
mcqs.explanation: The ICRC is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When was Transparency International founded?
mcqs.explanation: Transparency International was founded in May 1993 in Berlin.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of Transparency International?
mcqs.explanation: Transparency International is headquartered in Berlin, Germany.

mcqs.q: When was Amnesty International founded by Peter Benenson?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International was founded by Peter Benenson in July 1961.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of Amnesty International?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Islamic Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The Islamic Development Bank is headquartered in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: When did the International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL) come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL was founded in Vienna in 1923 (renamed in 1956).

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of INTERPOL?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL has its headquarters in Lyon, France.

mcqs.q: The Caribbean Community (CARICOM) was established on 1 August 1973 with its headquarters at:
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM is headquartered in Georgetown, Guyana.

mcqs.q: When was the Asian Development Bank established?
mcqs.explanation: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) was established on 19 December 1966.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Asian Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: As stated by the original source set, this MCQ keys to 'Nepal'. (Note: ADB's actual HQ is Manila, Philippines — answer kept as per the source bank.)

mcqs.q: When was RCD (Regional Cooperation for Development) established?
mcqs.explanation: RCD — predecessor of ECO — was established in 1964 between Iran, Pakistan and Turkey.

mcqs.q: What are the total members of the Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO)?
mcqs.explanation: ECO has 11 member states (added: Afghanistan, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan).

mcqs.q: Pakistan, Iran and Turkey re-established the Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO) for promoting technical, cultural and economic cooperation in:
mcqs.explanation: ECO was formally established in 1985 by Pakistan, Iran and Turkey.

mcqs.q: The Secretary-General of ECO is elected for a period of:
mcqs.explanation: The ECO Secretary-General is elected for a 3-year term.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO)?
mcqs.explanation: ECO's headquarters is in Tehran, Iran.

mcqs.q: When was the Colombo Plan founded?
mcqs.explanation: The Colombo Plan was founded in 1950.

mcqs.q: When was Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) formed?
mcqs.explanation: APEC was formed in 1989 in Canberra, Australia.

mcqs.q: OPEC, the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries, was founded on:
mcqs.explanation: OPEC was founded on 14 September 1960 in Baghdad.

mcqs.q: Where are the headquarters of the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC)?
mcqs.explanation: OPEC is headquartered in Vienna, Austria.

mcqs.q: What are the total members of OPEC?
mcqs.explanation: OPEC currently has 14 member states (numbers fluctuate as states join/leave).

mcqs.q: Which is the world's oldest international human rights organization (founded in 1839)?
mcqs.explanation: Anti-Slavery International, founded in 1839, is the world's oldest international human-rights organization.

mcqs.q: Americas Watch was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: Americas Watch was founded in 1981 (later merged into Human Rights Watch).

mcqs.q: Freedom House was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: Freedom House was founded in October 1941 in New York.

mcqs.q: Asia Watch was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: Asia Watch was founded in 1985 (later part of Human Rights Watch).

mcqs.q: The term 'Non-Alignment' itself was coined by:
mcqs.explanation: Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru coined the term 'Non-Alignment'.

mcqs.q: The Bandung Conference was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The Bandung (Asian-African) Conference was held in 1955 in Indonesia, paving the way for NAM.

mcqs.q: The first NAM Conference was held in Belgrade (Yugoslavia) in:
mcqs.explanation: The first NAM Summit was held in Belgrade in September 1961.

mcqs.q: Pakistan joined the NAM in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan formally joined the Non-Aligned Movement in 1979.

mcqs.q: Who was the first General Secretary of the Non-Aligned Movement?
mcqs.explanation: Yugoslav President Josip Broz Tito was a founder of NAM and its first General Secretary.

mcqs.q: The Arab League was established on:
mcqs.explanation: The Arab League was established on 22 March 1945 in Cairo.

mcqs.q: The headquarters of the Arab League is located at:
mcqs.explanation: The Arab League is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt.

mcqs.q: The Arab League is comprised of how many member countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab League has 22 member states.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan join the WTO?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan became a founding WTO member on 1 January 1995.

mcqs.q: When did the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) go into effect?
mcqs.explanation: GATT entered into force on 1 January 1948 (signed in October 1947).

mcqs.q: When did the World Trade Organization (WTO) come into effect?
mcqs.explanation: The WTO came into effect on 1 January 1995, replacing GATT.

mcqs.q: China became a member of the World Trade Organization (WTO) in:
mcqs.explanation: China joined the WTO on 11 December 2001.

mcqs.q: Russia became a member of the World Trade Organization (WTO) in:
mcqs.explanation: Russia joined the WTO on 22 August 2012.

mcqs.q: Scouts is a worldwide organized movement for young people, started in:
mcqs.explanation: As per this MCQ source, the 'Scouts' movement is recorded with the year 1917 (note: the international Boy Scouts movement was founded by Robert Baden-Powell in 1907).

mcqs.q: The Scouts Movement was founded by:
mcqs.explanation: The Scouts Movement was founded by Sir Robert Baden-Powell.

mcqs.q: The Group of 77 was established under the auspices of the UN in:
mcqs.explanation: The Group of 77 was established at UNCTAD in Geneva on 15 June 1964.

mcqs.q: The D-8 Summit is convened once every:
mcqs.explanation: The D-8 (Developing 8) Summit is normally convened every year.

mcqs.q: SAARC was formed in Dhaka on:
mcqs.explanation: The South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) was formed in Dhaka on 8 December 1985.

mcqs.q: The headquarters of SAARC is located in which city?
mcqs.explanation: SAARC is headquartered in Kathmandu, Nepal.

mcqs.q: Which SAARC summit was held in Islamabad in 1988?
mcqs.explanation: The 4th SAARC Summit was held in Islamabad in 1988.

mcqs.q: The first SAARC summit was held in 1985 in:
mcqs.explanation: The first SAARC summit was held in Dhaka in 1985.

mcqs.q: In the acronym SAARC, 'C' stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SAARC stands for South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation.

mcqs.q: Name the first Secretary-General of SAARC.
mcqs.explanation: Abu Saeed Hamiduddin Ahsan of Bangladesh was the first SG of SAARC.

mcqs.q: The Association of South-East Asian Nations (ASEAN) was established on 9 August 1967 with its headquarters in:
mcqs.explanation: ASEAN is headquartered in Jakarta, Indonesia.

mcqs.q: China founded the international alliance 'Shanghai Five' in:
mcqs.explanation: The 'Shanghai Five' alliance was founded in April 1996.

mcqs.q: In June 2001, which Central Asian state was invited to join the Shanghai Five, after which the group was officially named the SCO?
mcqs.explanation: Uzbekistan joined in June 2001, transforming the Shanghai Five into the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO).

mcqs.q: When was the Islamic Development Bank formed?
mcqs.explanation: The Islamic Development Bank was formed in August 1974.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC)?
mcqs.explanation: The OIC is headquartered in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: When was the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) established?
mcqs.explanation: OIC was established in September 1969 after the Al-Aqsa fire.

mcqs.q: What are the total members of the OIC?
mcqs.explanation: The OIC has 57 member states.

mcqs.q: The headquarters of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The OIC headquarters is in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) was established on:
mcqs.explanation: The OPCW was established on 29 April 1997 (Chemical Weapons Convention entry into force).

mcqs.q: Where is the head office of the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW), the 2013 Nobel Peace Prize laureate?
mcqs.explanation: OPCW is headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands; it won the Nobel Peace Prize in 2013.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Worldwide Fund for Nature (WWF)?
mcqs.explanation: WWF is headquartered in Gland, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When did the Antarctic Treaty come into force?
mcqs.explanation: The Antarctic Treaty entered into force on 23 June 1961.

mcqs.q: When did North Korea and South Korea agree to resume reunions of war-split families?
mcqs.explanation: On 5 February 2014, the two Koreas agreed to resume reunions of families separated by the Korean War.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of EFTA located?
mcqs.explanation: The European Free Trade Association (EFTA) — established 3 May 1960 — is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.
